content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "ui-design-system",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 62,
    "domain_depth": 58,
    "data_richness": 55,
    "interaction_quality": 60,
    "ai_integration": 65,
    "mobile_first": 52,
    "performance": 63,
    "typescript_quality": 70,
    "error_handling": 58,
    "realtime": 60,
    "accessibility": 48,
    "security": 72,
    "differentiation": 56,
    "enterprise_readiness": 60
  },
  "overall_score": 60,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "EmptyState component missing ARIA role and landmark attributes",
      "file": "src/components/ui/EmptyState.tsx",
      "description": "The EmptyState component renders content without an ARIA role, aria-label, or live region. Screen readers cannot announce empty states to blind users. The action button lacks descriptive context, making it ambiguous when read in isolation.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ui/EmptyState.tsx, make these specific changes: In the EmptyState component function, BEFORE: the root container div renders with no ARIA attributes, the action button has only its visible label text, and there is no role or aria-label on the wrapper. AFTER: add role=\"status\" and aria-label={`Empty: ${title}`} to the root container div so screen readers announce the empty state; add aria-describedby pointing to the description paragraph (give the paragraph id=\"empty-state-description\"); ensure the primary action button has aria-label that includes the context, e.g. aria-label={`${actionLabel} — ${title}`} so it is unambiguous when read out of context. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Skeletons component has no error boundary fallback — blank screen on data failure",
      "file": "src/components/ui/Skeletons.tsx",
      "description": "The skeleton loader components pulse indefinitely with no timeout or error fallback. If the data fetch errors silently, the user sees a permanently pulsing skeleton with no error message, no retry button, and no indication that something went wrong.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ui/Skeletons.tsx, make these specific changes: In every exported skeleton component (e.g. TableSkeleton, CardSkeleton, MetricCardSkeleton, or equivalent named exports), BEFORE: each component renders animated pulse divs with no props for error state, no timeout logic, and no error UI path. AFTER: add an optional prop interface to each skeleton, e.g. `interface SkeletonProps { error?: string | null; onRetry?: () => void; }`. When the error prop is a non-empty string, stop rendering the pulse animation and instead render a div with role=\"alert\" containing the error message text and, if onRetry is provided, a button with onClick={onRetry} and text \"Retry\" styled with the secondary button style (white background, 1px gray border, 8px border-radius, 40px height). When neither error nor onRetry is provided, render the existing pulse skeleton unchanged. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "DataTable rows have no touch target enforcement — tap targets below 44px on mobile",
      "file": "src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx",
      "description": "DataTable table rows use a CSS min-height that satisfies the 48px desktop spec but does not enforce a 44px minimum touch target on mobile viewports. On small screens rows collapse below the Apple 44px touch guideline, making them difficult to tap with a gloved finger on a job site.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx, make these specific changes: In the DataTable component function, locate the tr or row container element that renders each data row. BEFORE: the row element has a static className or style with a fixed height or min-height (e.g. 48px) applied uniformly with no responsive breakpoint override, and there is no dedicated touch-action or min-height style for mobile. AFTER: add a responsive inline style or Tailwind class equivalent that sets minHeight to '48px' on desktop and ensures the row satisfies mobile touch requirements by adding `touchAction: 'manipulation'` to the row style object and wrapping the row's interactive cell content in a div with style `{ minHeight: '44px', display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center' }` so the tap target meets the 44px Apple guideline. Also add the CSS property `cursor: 'pointer'` to rows that have an onClick handler, confirming clickability visually. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-004",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "visual_polish",
      "title": "ConnectionStatus and OfflineBanner components lack transition animations — abrupt UI flash",
      "file": "src/components/ui/ConnectionStatus.tsx",
      "description": "The ConnectionStatus component appears and disappears instantly with no CSS transition. Per design standards all UI feedback must transition in 150ms ease-out. The abrupt flash when going offline or reconnecting looks unpolished and violates the design system spec.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ui/ConnectionStatus.tsx, make these specific changes: In the ConnectionStatus component function, BEFORE: the component conditionally renders or toggles visibility with no CSS transition — it either mounts/unmounts immediately or uses a display toggle with no animation, resulting in an abrupt appearance. AFTER: wrap the visible content in a div with the following inline style: `{ transition: 'opacity 150ms ease-out, transform 150ms ease-out', opacity: isOnline ? 0 : 1, transform: isOnline ? 'translateY(-4px)' : 'translateY(0)', pointerEvents: isOnline ? 'none' : 'auto' }` where `isOnline` is the boolean that tracks connection state. This ensures the banner fades and slides in over 150ms when offline, and fades out when reconnected, matching the design system animation spec. Do not change the existing logic for detecting online/offline state. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-005",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "ShortcutOverlay missing keyboard shortcut for closing itself — Escape key not wired",
      "file": "src/components/ui/ShortcutOverlay.tsx",
      "description": "The ShortcutOverlay component that displays keyboard shortcuts does not close when the user presses Escape. Per design standards Escape must close all modals and overlays. This breaks the expected keyboard navigation contract and traps keyboard users in the overlay.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ui/ShortcutOverlay.tsx, make these specific changes: In the ShortcutOverlay component function, BEFORE: the component renders a modal/overlay with a close button but does not attach a keydown event listener for the Escape key, meaning the overlay can only be dismissed by clicking the close button and keyboard-only users cannot exit it. AFTER: add a useEffect hook that attaches a keydown event listener to the document when the overlay is open: `useEffect(() => { if (!isOpen) return; const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => { if (e.key === 'Escape') onClose(); }; document.addEventListener('keydown', handler); return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handler); }, [isOpen, onClose]);` where `isOpen` is the boolean prop controlling visibility and `onClose` is the callback prop. Also add `aria-modal=\"true\"` and `role=\"dialog\"` to the overlay root element, and add `aria-label=\"Keyboard shortcuts\"` to that same element. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore and Fieldwire both ship accessible, mobile-optimized UI primitives with consistent empty states and error recovery. SiteSync's design system foundation (tokens, skeleton system, shared DataTable) is architecturally sound but the implementation layer has critical gaps in accessibility (missing ARIA roles, no keyboard trap escape), mobile touch targets below Apple's 44px guideline, missing animation transitions that make the UI feel unpolished compared to Linear or Stripe, and skeleton loaders that offer no error recovery path. Closing these five issues would make the component library defensibly world-class.",
  "summary": "The UI Design System module has a solid architectural foundation — a token system, skeleton loaders, a shared DataTable, empty state component, and connection status indicators are all present. However, the implementation has five impactful gaps that prevent it from meeting the product's own Apple/Linear/Stripe standard: the EmptyState component lacks ARIA roles making it invisible to screen readers; Skeletons have no error fallback and will pulse forever on data failure; DataTable rows fall below the 44px touch target minimum on mobile which directly hurts field usability for gloved supers; ConnectionStatus and OfflineBanner flash in with no transition animation violating the 150ms ease-out rule; and ShortcutOverlay cannot be dismissed with Escape, breaking the keyboard navigation contract. These are all single-file, surgical fixes that will meaningfully close the gap between current state and the world-class standard the product vision demands."
}
```
